rulings.Case Title: ROSA ANN ROBINSON VS. JEFFERY WINDHAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff For Assignment Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF ROSA ROBINSON's Motion By Plaintiff For Assignment Order OFF CALENDAR. Enforcement of judgment and assignment order motions are heard in Department 514. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA Q. RODRIGUEZ VS. DORA E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 2. Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6 Hearing Required= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VERA V WITIUK VS. JANE KNOP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK'S MOTION To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint continued to May 21, 2019. By no later than May 14, 2019 moving party to provide a supplemental brief and declaration addressing the following: (1) based on the size of the estate does decedent's will have to be probated; (2) if so, under what law the will shall be probated, i.e. where was the decedent domiciled at the time of her death for the purposes of probate; (3) whether decedent's estate had been/is being administered within or without California. Additionally, Moving parties to provide a copy of the will for in camera review. Opposing party may submit a supplemental opposition no later than May 17, 2019. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DENIS CASEY VS. JACK MESA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT STEPHANIE GRAHAM's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. A relationship between the landlord and Stephanie Graham is sufficiently alleged to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DENIS CASEY VS. JACK MESA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT JACK MESA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Legal effect of the contract is sufficiently alleged for the purposes of stating an unlawful detainer cause of action. It does not appear on the face of the complaint that a portion which may be material to the nuisance unlawful detainer has been omitted. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID TSAO VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC'S. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication GRANTED, notice has been waived by acceptance of rent. Paragraph 23 of Plaintiff's declaration does not provide sufficient admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact re: waiver.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. GUI HE LI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT GUI LI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer as it not uncertain. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SHUN YOU SUN VS. MARIA TOJIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 08, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT MARIA TOJIN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on Ellis Act. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the determinative date for the purposes of additional relocation payment determination is the date of the service of the notice of termination. Form 578 is not authority. Additionally, no authority is cited for the proposition that overpayment constitutes failure to comply with Ellis Act or SFRO. Complaint sufficiently alleges that there were 5 separate tenancies at the premises, as a result allegedly creating five separate residential units (regardless of their legal status and zoning). Nothing on the face of the complaint or judicially noticeable documents indicate that occupants of rooms 1 and 3 at 2820 Folsom had tenancies that included Room 2, that would allow for a 1-year extension to be applicable to room 2. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that every attempted subsection (f)(1) notice triggers landlord's obligations under SFRO 37.9A(f)(5). Whether the first notice was an effective compliant subsection (f)(1) notice and whether it triggered (f)(5) obligations is not an issue that may be addressed on a demurrer as it requires a factual inquiry in addition to procedural and legal determinations. = (501/CFH)
